title: The Midnight Serenade
setting: The Lumina Gallery — a prestigious private art gallery
premise: A priceless masterpiece, 'Midnight Serenade', has been stolen from the highly secure private vault at the Lumina Gallery. In its place lies a masterful forgery. Simultaneously, a young, innocent child has been found unconscious in the gallery courtyard. The security logs show no alarms, and the biometric entries reveal only routine personnel visits. This is an inside job.

suspects.name: Eleanor Voss
suspects.alibi: I left the building at 11:00 PM according to the front desk sign-out sheet, before the vault was even sealed for the night.
suspects.secret: She is terrified of professional ruin and came back at 1:00 AM to fetch her forgotten planner, but she insists she was home all night.
suspects.contradiction: She insists she was home all night after 11 PM, but she is highly defensive and hiding her brief middle-of-the-night return.
suspects.verbal_tell: adjusts her pearl necklace nervously whenever her departure time is brought up
suspects.unrelated_secret: She is secretly interviewing for a director position at a rival gallery in London.

suspects.name: Rosa Delgado
suspects.alibi: I was cleaning the east wing, on the opposite side of the building, from 2:30 AM to 3:15 AM.
suspects.secret: She found a discarded prototype frame in the east wing trash and took it, fearing she would be blamed for stealing.
suspects.contradiction: Her shift log places her in the east wing during the heist, confirming she had no access to the vault.
suspects.verbal_tell: crosses her arms tightly and sighs heavily whenever shifts are mentioned
suspects.unrelated_secret: She takes leftover gourmet catering food home to feed her family.

suspects.name: Priya Kapoor
suspects.alibi: I was on a phone call with the family's driver from 2:45 AM to 3:05 AM, confirmed by call records.
suspects.secret: She fell asleep for 10 minutes before her phone call and feels immense guilt for leaving the child unattended.
suspects.contradiction: Her phone call records confirm her alibi during the exact time of the alarm.
suspects.verbal_tell: speaks in a rapid, hushed stammer and wrings her hands constantly
suspects.unrelated_secret: She was secretly texting the driver about plans to find a new job.

suspects.name: Marcus Reyes
suspects.alibi: I was in the security booth monitoring cameras all night, except for a routine maintenance check of the west wing.
suspects.secret: He was bribed by a private art collector to swap the painting for a forgery, and used the child's routine vault visit as cover. When the child startled him, he pushed and restrained them to avoid discovery.
suspects.contradiction: His badge log reveals he disabled the west wing motion sensors at 2:50 AM, right before the heist, contradicting a standard non-disabling patrol.
suspects.verbal_tell: speaks in flat, overly formal military protocol and maintains unblinking eye contact
suspects.unrelated_secret: He has a chronic gambling debt of seventy thousand dollars.

Vault.name: Vault
Vault.description: The private viewing vault. Biometric scanner still blinking green — it never flagged the swap.

Storage_Room.name: Storage Room
Storage_Room.description: Where the child was found. A single overturned display crate lies nearby.

Security_Booth.name: Security Booth
Security_Booth.description: Bank of monitors, mostly dark screens for the west wing.

Front_Desk.name: Front Desk
Front_Desk.description: Visitor sign-out sheet still open on the counter.

West_Wing.name: West Wing
West_Wing.description: Motion sensor housing left slightly ajar on one panel.

Curators_Office.name: Curator's Office
Curators_Office.description: Neat desk, a half-drafted resignation letter tucked under a folder.

Break_Room.name: Break Room
Break_Room.description: A phone charger still plugged in at the corner table.

Main_Hall.name: Main Hall
Main_Hall.description: Where the family and staff gathered after the alarm.

clues.vault_scanner_log: The biometric log shows only the child's badge that night — no other entries, meaning whoever swapped the painting either had no badge on file or bypassed the scanner entirely.
clues.storage_crate: The crate was used for shipping the original painting in — its lid was pried open recently, consistent with concealing the real canvas during transport out.
clues.storage_footprint: A single partial boot print near the crate, size and tread inconsistent with the family or the nanny's footwear.
clues.security_maintenance_note: A logged 'routine maintenance' entry for the west wing sensors at 2:50 AM — no maintenance ticket exists in the system for that night.
clues.signout_sheet: Gallery_Curator's signature confirms she left at 11:00 PM, well before the vault was sealed.
clues.cleaning_schedule: Night_Cleaner's assigned zone that night was strictly the east wing — corroborated by a supervisor's handwritten schedule.
clues.sensor_panel: The panel shows manual override markings, requiring physical keycard access — not remote tampering.
clues.draft_letter: An unfinished resignation letter, dated a week ago, addressed to a rival gallery. Unrelated to the theft but explains Gallery_Curator's nervous evasiveness.
clues.nanny_phone_log: Call records confirm Family_Nanny was on the phone with the family driver from 2:45 AM to 3:05 AM.
